methods.method_name: Sequence Data Quality Control and Processing
methods.description: This method involves quality control and processing of raw sequence data obtained from Illumina MiSeq to ensure high-quality sequences for clustering.

steps.description: Quality control of raw fastq data files was performed using FastQC v0.11.4.
steps.searchable_summary: perform quality control on raw fastq data using FastQC

steps.description: High-throughput sequence raw data were imported into CLC Genomics Workbench version 9.0.1 with plugins CLC Microbial Genomics Module version 1.3.1 according to quality scores from Illumina pipeline 1.8.
steps.searchable_summary: import sequence data into CLC Genomics Workbench with quality score filtering

steps.description: Sequence data were trimmed using 0.05 as a limit for quality scores with 2 as the maximum number of ambiguities.
steps.searchable_summary: trim sequence data based on quality scores and ambiguities

steps.description: The optional merge paired reads method was run with default settings.
steps.searchable_summary: merge paired reads with default settings

steps.description: Sequence reads were clustered and chimeric sequences detected using an identity of 97% as the OTU threshold.
steps.searchable_summary: cluster sequence reads and detect chimeric sequences with 97% identity threshold

methods.description: This method involves downloading reference OTU data from various databases for different microbial communities.

steps.description: Reference OTU data used in the present study were downloaded from the Greengenes database for 16S rRNA, the Unite database for ITS, and Silva for 18S rRNA.
steps.searchable_summary: download OTU reference data from Greengenes, Unite, and Silva databases

methods.description: This method estimates the relative abundances of OTUs at the family level for different microbial communities across sediment layers.

steps.description: Relative abundances of the most abundant OTUs among all four-sediment layers for all 10 cores were estimated at the family level for archaea, bacteria, eukaryotes, and fungi.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate OTU relative abundances at family level across sediment layers

methods.description: This method processes and analyzes LC-MS raw data to screen methanol extracts for chemodiversity without focusing on specific metabolites.

steps.description: LC-MS raw data were processed and analyzed using Mzmine version 2.02, XCMS R package, and MetaboAnalyst version 3.0.
steps.searchable_summary: process and analyze LC-MS data using Mzmine, XCMS, and MetaboAnalyst

steps.description: Selected the most significant MS signals based on p-values (p ≤ 0.01) and Relative Standard Deviations (RSD ≤ 10%) among layers of sampled cores.
steps.searchable_summary: select significant MS signals based on p-values and RSD

methods.description: This method involves various statistical analyses to estimate diversity and compare microbial communities across different layers and cores.

steps.description: Good's estimator of coverage and Shannon's entropy were estimated for all microbial communities.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate Good's coverage and Shannon's entropy for microbial communities

steps.description: A PERMANOVA test was conducted based on Bray-Curtis and Weighted UniFrac distances among cores.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct PERMANOVA test using Bray-Curtis and Weighted UniFrac distances

steps.description: Principal Coordinates Analysis (PCoA) was used to characterize all layers and the top layer among cores.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCoA to characterize layers among cores

steps.description: Alpha diversity (rarefaction curves) was calculated based on Chao 1 measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate alpha diversity using Chao 1 measurements

steps.description: Partial Least Squares Discriminant Analysis (PLS) estimated the weighted sum of absolute regression coefficients among the microbial communities.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PLS to estimate regression coefficients among microbial communities

steps.description: The heatmap visualization was estimated based on Euclidean matrix distances, and Ward's clustering algorithm was utilized.
steps.searchable_summary: create heatmap using Euclidean distances and Ward's clustering
